Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 714 upvotes, 69 comments
  2. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 198 upvotes, 37 comments
  3. A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 92 upvotes, 14 comments
  4. Using calculus to do number theory 67 upvotes, 10 comments
  5. Mistral AI Releases Forge 682 upvotes, 173 comments
  6. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 387 upvotes, 107 comments
  7. Americans Recognize AI as a Wealth Inequality Machine, Polls Find 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  8. Snowflake AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware 152 upvotes, 40 comments
  9. OpenRocket 80 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 96 upvotes, 26 comments
  11. Show HN: Hacker News archive (47M+ items, 11.6GB) as Parquet, updated every 5m 73 upvotes, 19 comments
  12. The pleasures of poor product design 222 upvotes, 77 comments
  13. Oil nears $110 a barrel after gas field strike 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 292 upvotes, 125 comments
  15. 2025 Turing award given for quantum information science 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Google Engineers Launch "Sashiko" for Agentic AI Code Review of the Linux Kernel 45 upvotes, 15 comments
  17. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool (just 2 files) to explore the small web 38 upvotes, 12 comments
  18. Death to Scroll Fade 237 upvotes, 117 comments
  19. Rob Pike's Rules of Programming (1989) 619 upvotes, 339 comments
  20. Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) 81 upvotes, 38 comments
  21. Restoring the first recording of computer music (2018) 21 upvotes, 8 comments
  22. Nvidia NemoClaw 96 upvotes, 59 comments
  23. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 50 upvotes, 32 comments
  24. CVE-2026-3888: Important Snap Flaw Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. AI Coding Is Gambling 92 upvotes, 73 comments
  26. A dither generator for triangular and hexagonal pixels (2025) 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Show HN: Tmux-IDE, OSS agent-first terminal IDE 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. North Korean's 100k fake IT workers net $500M a year for Kim 78 upvotes, 76 comments
  29. I haven't used a mouse for 14 years 13 upvotes, 22 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments
